zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joan Brydges de Brugge於1503年出生在英國波克夏紐波利時，她的父親Henry Brydges28歲，母親Margery Buckerd20歲。她於1520年與Sir John Henry Gifford在英國波克夏紐波利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1572年在英國蘇格蘭洛迪安享年69歲，埋葬在英國哈普爾易欽。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Joan Brydges de Brugge于1503年出生在英国波克夏紐波利时，她的父亲Henry Brydges28岁，母亲Margery Buckerd20岁。她于1520年与Sir John Henry Gifford在英国波克夏紐波利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1572年在英国苏格兰洛迪安享年69岁，埋葬在英国哈普爾易欽。

nameMeanings.name: Brydges
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernd.
English: variant of   Bridges  .

variants.name: Bridges
meanings: English: variant of Bridge . The -s generally represents the genitive case, but may occasionally be a plural. In some cases this name denoted someone from the Flemish city of Bruges (Brugge) in Belgium, meaning ‘bridges’, which had extensive trading links with England in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brugge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch (Van der Brugge): topographic name for someone living near a brug ‘bridge’.
Flemish and Dutch: habitational name from Bruges.
North German (Brügge): variant of Bruck or a habitational name from any of several places called Brügge.

variants.name: Bruck
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Naftali, Eliahu, Hershel, Meyer, Ruvim, Shloime, Yoav.

German (also Brück): topographic name for someone who lived near a bridge, or an occupational name for a bridge keeper or toll collector on a bridge, from Middle High German bruck(e) ‘bridge’. Bruck remained the Bavarian and Austrian form, reflected in placenames such as Innsbruck, whereas in central and northern Germany Brück(e) was the regular and ultimately standard form, as for example in the placename Osnabrück. Switzerland has both forms. See also Brueck .
Jewish (from Poland and Belarus): from Polish, Belorussian, or Yiddish bruk ‘pavement’, possibly a metonymic occupational name for a paver.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joan Brydges de Brugge was born in 1503, in Newbury, Berkshire, England, her father, Henry Brydges, was 28 and her mother, Margery Buckerd, was 20. She married Sir John Henry Gifford in 1520, in Newbury, Berkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. She died in 1572, in Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 69, and was buried in Itchen, Hampshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Joan Brydges de Brugge est née en 1503, à Newbury, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry Brydges, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margery Buckerd, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Henry Gifford en 1520, à Newbury, Berkshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée en 1572, à Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Itchen, Hampshire, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1520 in Newbury, Berkshire, England Sir John Henry Gifford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb 1572 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Itchen, Hampshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Brydges de Bruggeが1503年にイギリスバークシュアイアニューブリーで生まれた時，父Henry Brydgesは28歳，母Margery Buckerdは20歳であった。1520年にイギリスバークシュアイアニューブリーでSir John Henry Giffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1572年にイギリススコットランドロスアンで69歳で死去し，イギリスハンプシュアイアイトチェンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Brydges de Brugge(이)가 1503년 영국 버크실르 뉴버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Brydges(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Buckerd(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1520년 영국 버크실르 뉴버이에서 Sir John Henry Gifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1572년 영국 스코틀랜드 로스이언에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 함프실르 잇친에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Joan Brydges de Brugge nasceu em 1503, em Newbury, Berkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Brydges, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Margery Buckerd, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Henry Gifford em 1520, em Newbury, Berkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1572, em Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Itchen, Hampshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Joan Brydges de Brugge родилась в 1503 г. в местности нюбури, биркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry Brydges было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Buckerd, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Henry Gifford в 1520 г. в местности нюбури, биркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла в 1572 г. в местности лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности ичэн, хампшир, Англия.

es.Donhead, Wiltshire, England: Donhead St Andrew, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Joan Brydges de Brugge nació en 1503, en Newbury, Berkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Brydges, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Margery Buckerd, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Sir John Henry Gifford en 1520, en Newbury, Berkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió en 1572, en Lothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Itchen, Hampshire, Inglaterra.
